sections.heading: Risk Factors
sections.content:
Phimosis: Tight foreskin that cannot be retracted over the glans, hindering hygiene and potentially trapping HPV. Increased risk of penile cancer by 25% to 60%. [4,10,11]
Human papillomavirus (HPV) infection: Infection with high-risk HPV types, especially 16, 6, and 18, is strongly associated with penile intraepithelial neoplasia (PeIN) and SCC. Found in 45% to 80% of penile cancers. HPV or p16 positivity is a favorable prognostic factor. [4,12,13,14,15,16,17]
Human immunodeficiency virus (HIV) infection: Increases the risk of HPV infection and associated disease due to immunosuppression. HIV-positive individuals have a higher risk of HPV acquisition and lower HPV clearance. [18,19]
Tobacco use: Smoking cigarettes is a significant risk factor. Makes individuals 3 to 4.5 times more likely to develop penile cancer. [12,22]
Lichen sclerosus: A chronic inflammatory skin condition affecting the genital area. Associated with a 2% to 9% risk of developing penile carcinoma. [23,24,25]
Balanitis and chronic inflammation: Inflammation of the glans penis, often due to poor hygiene or infection. Listed as a risk factor; strength of association not quantified. [4]
Lack of neonatal circumcision: Being uncircumcised at birth increases risk, likely due to phimosis and HPV retention. Neonatal circumcision is associated with a lower rate of penile cancer. [4,14,20]
Poor hygiene: Inadequate genital hygiene may contribute to smegma accumulation and chronic irritation. Listed as a risk factor; strength of association not quantified. [4]
Sexually transmitted diseases: History of sexually transmitted infections, particularly HPV and HIV. Listed as a risk factor; strength of association not quantified. [4]
Penile trauma: History of injury to the penis. Listed as a risk factor; strength of association not quantified. [4]
Psoriasis treatment with psoralen plus ultraviolet A (PUVA): Patients with psoriasis undergoing PUVA therapy have markedly increased risk. Incidence increased 286-fold compared to the general population. [26]
